  do not introduce new security risks and configurations remain secure.

> [!NOTE]
> Further information and key documentation:
> [RISM08 Change Management Policy](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism08-change_management_policy/)
> [RISM04 Risk Assessment and Treatment Procedure](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism04-risk_assessment_and_treatment_procedure/)
> [RISM12 Environment Definition](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism12-environment_definition/)
> [RISM18 GitOps Procedure](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism18-gitops_procedure/)

> [!TIP]
> Use this template for significant [Normal or Emergency Changes](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism08-change_management_policy/#4-change-types) to the ISMS or to environments within its scope.  
  
> A significant change is any planned or unplanned modification that could materially affect the information-security risk posture. This includes technical, procedural, or architectural changes—for example, updates to critical systems, policies, infrastructure, or responsibilities. 

> These changes are typically identified as having a **High** or **Very High** impact rating and require formal ISMS assessment, oversight, and approval by the [ISMS Operational Management Group (OMG)](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism11-operational_management_group_tor/). 

>OMG ensures the ISMS is operating effectively, delivering security objectives, and managing information risks within tolerance.  
  
> Risks that cannot be resolved by OMG may be escalated to the [UCL Information Risk Governance Committee (IRGC)](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism02-roles_and_responsibilities/#31-top-management).  
  
> Arrangements for OMG and IRGC are coordinated through the [ISMS Research Information Governance (IG) Lead](https://isms.arc.ucl.ac.uk/rism02-roles_and_responsibilities/#8roles-catalogue).

***
